title: At-least-once, at-most-once, exactly-once
description: Brokers practically offer at-least-once; exactly-once processing is an application property built from idempotency.
body: Use when choosing how much duplication and loss to tolerate. At-most-once acks before processing (can lose events); at-least-once processes before acking (can duplicate, the common safe default); 'exactly-once' end to end requires either transactional broker features within one system or, more portably, idempotent consumers. Do not assume a broker's exactly-once flag extends to your external side effects. Actions: default to at-least-once + idempotent consumers; reserve broker transactions for in-broker read-process-write. Validation signals include a redelivery test yielding one effect. Risk boundary: choosing at-most-once for a flow that cannot lose events is a silent data-loss risk.
